title: Boat Rental in Tahiti: Costs and Tips for 2026
html:
Renting a boat in Tahiti typically costs from €150 for a half-day motorboat to over €1,200 for a crewed yacht, with departure points around Papeete and the Society Islands. BoatHire24 connects you with verified local operators across the lagoon and open sea.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Tahiti?

Boat rental in Tahiti starts at around €150 for a small motorboat and reaches €2,500 for a crewed superyacht per day. The price depends on boat type, season, and whether you hire a skipper. You can compare options and book a boat online through platforms like BoatHire24, which lists vessels from private owners and charter companies. One option worth a look is the Mefasa 90 for hire.

For a typical half-day lagoon tour on a 6-metre RIB, expect to pay between €150 and €300. Full-day catamaran charters for up to ten guests range from €800 to €1,500, including crew and fuel. Luxury motor yachts like the Mefasa 90 for hire cost upwards of €2,000 per day with full service.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Tahiti or list your own. The site lets you filter by price, capacity, and licence requirements, making it straightforward to find a boat that fits your budget.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Tahiti?

For most small motorboats under 10 metres, you do not need a licence, but for larger vessels a valid International Certificate of Competence is required. French Polynesia follows French maritime law, so operators will confirm exact requirements at the time of booking. Many rental boats come with a skipper included, removing the need for a licence altogether.

If you plan to explore the outer reefs around Moorea, book a boat with a local skipper who knows the passes. The tides and currents can change quickly, and a good skipper will take you to the best snorkelling spots.

Where to set off in Tahiti

Most boat rentals depart from Papeete and its main marinas: Marina Taina, Marina Arue, and the city port. These are close to the airport and offer easy access to the lagoon and open sea. From here, you can cruise to islands such as Moorea, which sits just 17 nautical miles away, or head to Tahiti Iti for quieter anchorages.

Other popular departure points include the marina at Vaiare in Moorea, the harbour in Uturoa on Raiatea, and the sheltered bays of Bora Bora such as Povai Bay and the Matira Point area. For sailing enthusiasts, the channel between Tahiti and Moorea is a reliable passage with steady trade winds.

You can book a boat online that departs from any of these locations, with operators listing exact meeting points and itineraries.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat (4, 6 metres), ideal for exploring the lagoon and visiting motus, suitable for 4, 6 guests, from €150 for half a day.
  2. Cabin cruiser (8, 10 metres), comfortable for a day trip or overnight, sleeps 4, 6, from €400 for a full day.
  3. Catamaran (12, 15 metres), perfect for groups and families, stable sailing, capacity up to 12 guests, from €800 per day.
  4. Crewed motor yacht, luxury experience with captain and host, such as the Mefasa 90 for hire, sleeps 10, from €2,000 per day.

Popular models available include the Lagoon 450 catamaran for sailing charters and the Axopar 28 for fast day trips. The Mefasa 90 for hire in Tahiti is a notable option for those seeking a premium experience with ample deck space and water toys.

Seasonality and costs

High season in Tahiti runs from May to October, when the trade winds provide consistent sailing conditions and the weather is drier. During these months, boat rental prices can be 20, 30% higher than in the low season (November to April). The prevailing wind, known locally as the Maramu, blows from the east-southeast and can reach 20 knots in winter.

  • Shared lagoon tour: €80, €120 per person for a half-day excursion
  • Small motorboat half-day: €150, €300 for up to 6 guests, no licence needed
  • Catamaran full-day: €800, €1,500 for up to 12 guests, with skipper
  • Crewed luxury yacht: €1,200, €2,500 per day, all inclusive

For practical tips on the reservation process, read how boat rental works on BoatHire24, which explains booking steps, payment security, and cancellation policies.

At a glance

  • Price range: €150 to €2,500 per day
  • Peak months: May to October
  • Trip length: half-day, full-day, or multi-day
  • Typical capacity: up to 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR (euros widely accepted)

Questions about boat rental in Tahiti

Can I rent a boat in Tahiti without a licence?

Yes, you can rent small motorboats under 10 metres without a licence, and any boat can be chartered with a skipper.

What is the best time of year to rent a boat in Tahiti?

The best time is from May to October, when the weather is drier and the trade winds make sailing comfortable.

Do I need to bring my own snorkelling gear?

Most charter boats include snorkelling equipment, but it is wise to confirm with the operator when you book.

Can I rent a boat for a multi-day trip to nearby islands?

Yes, many catamarans and yachts are available for multi-day charters to Moorea, Raiatea, and Bora Bora.

Whether you are planning a day on the lagoon or a week-long sailing adventure, renting a boat in Tahiti opens up the Society Islands. If you own a boat, you can list it on BoatHire24 to reach thousands of travellers. To start, check availability and browse verified listings for your ideal trip.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Tahiti and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
